resources.string: Active capability tools are hidden while a scheduled task is being delivered.
Proactive capability is disabled in this config profile.
Error: %1$s
Schedule Future Task

Required for creating reminders, timers, timed follow-ups, and future proactive actions. Call this tool before telling the user a reminder or scheduled task has been set. Provide note as the core instruction, with cron_expression for recurring or run_at (ISO 8601) for one-time tasks.
Cancel Future Task
Cancel and delete a previously scheduled future task.
List Future Tasks
List all scheduled future tasks with status, next run time, and execution summary.
Pause Future Task
Pause a scheduled future task without deleting it.
Resume Future Task
Resume a paused scheduled future task.
List Future Task Runs
List recent execution records for a scheduled future task.
Update Future Task
Update conservative fields on a scheduled future task without changing its target context.
Run Future Task Now
Run a scheduled future task immediately through the injected scheduled task runner.
The job_id of the task to cancel.
The job_id of the task.
Maximum number of recent runs to return.
Updated short title.
Updated task instruction.
Whether the task is enabled.
Updated task status, for example scheduled or paused.
Updated ISO 8601 one-time run time.
Updated cron expression for recurring tasks.
Updated IANA timezone.
Run the task only once.
Optional short title for the task.
Core instruction for the reminder, timed follow-up, or future action.
Cron expression for recurring tasks.
ISO 8601 datetime for one-time execution.
Backward-compatible conversation or session id; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
IANA timezone, for example Asia/Shanghai.
Whether the task should be scheduled immediately.
If true, a past one-time schedule is accepted and runs immediately.
Runtime platform; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Target conversation id; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Target bot id; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Target config profile id; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Optional persona id override; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Target provider id; usually auto-filled from the current runtime context.
Creator origin, for example tool, api, or ui.
Unnamed Task
note is required for a future task and should describe the reminder or future action.
Either cron_expression or run_at must be provided. Relative time expressions such as 5 minutes later are only supported when they can be inferred from the current request.
Scheduled task time is in the past. Set allow_past_immediate=true to run it immediately.
Scheduled task target is incomplete: %1$s
job_id is required to delete a task.
job_id is required to update a task.
job_id is required to pause a task.
job_id is required to resume a task.
job_id is required to list task runs.
job_id is required to run a task now.
No scheduled task runner is available in this runtime.
Task with job_id %1$s not found.
OK, I have set the reminder.
That time has already passed, so I did not create the reminder.
The reminder was not created: %1$s
Remind the user to
host_scheduled_task_fallback: created\njob_id: %1$s\nTell the user naturally that the reminder was created. Do not call create_future_task again.

host_scheduled_task_fallback: failed
error_code: %1$s
message: %2$s
Tell the user naturally that the reminder was not created. Do not call create_future_task again.
